commands.review_summary:
The PR changes four cask `depends_on macos:` declarations from comparison strings to release symbols for blackbar, codexbar, repobar, and trimmy.

Reproducibility: no. this review did not reproduce the warning through a real Homebrew invocation. Source inspection confirms the four comparison-string requirements on current main, so the reported cleanup is source-reproducible but still needs after-fix proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for real behavior proof before merge; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.

target.title: fix(casks): use symbol form for depends_on macos
target.body:
## What

Replace the deprecated string-comparison form of `depends_on macos:` with the symbol form across four casks:

| Cask | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| blackbar | `depends_on macos: ">= :sonoma"` | `depends_on macos: :sonoma` |
| codexbar | `depends_on macos: ">= :sonoma"` | `depends_on macos: :sonoma` |
| repobar | `depends_on macos: ">= :sequoia"` | `depends_on macos: :sequoia` |
| trimmy | `depends_on macos: ">= :sequoia"` | `depends_on macos: :sequoia` |

## Why

Homebrew now emits a deprecation warning on every operation that touches the tap:

```
Warning: Calling string comparison format for `depends_on macos:` is deprecated! Use `depends_on macos: :sonoma` instead.
```

The bare symbol form already means ">= that version", so this is semantics-preserving — it just silences the warning.
target.author: donoage
